title: Introduction to `rome`
text: rome minimal R package read write multiscale OME-ZARR images. also provides helper methods manipulate resulting ome_zarr objects way one manipulate traditional arrays R. example, can subset ome_zarr object using [ operator, subsetting applied levels multiscale OME-Zarr object.

title: Introduction to `rome`
text: can install development version rome like :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("Huber-group-EMBL/rome")

title: Introduction to `rome`
text: basic example shows read OME-ZARR image version 0.4. default, read performed lazily using ZarrArray.  Otherwise read can performed memory :
code: library(rome) library(utils) omezarrzip <- system.file("extdata", "test_ngff_image_v04.ome.zarr.zip", package = "rome") dir.create(td <- tempfile()) unzip(omezarrzip, exdir = td) x <- ome_read(td) plot(x, 1) x <- ome_read(td, lazy = FALSE)

title: Introduction to `rome`
text: Labels image pyramids can also read images
code: omezarrzip <- system.file("extdata", "test_ngff_image_v04.ome.zarr.zip", package = "rome") dir.create(td <- tempfile()) unzip(omezarrzip, exdir = td) x <- ome_read(file.path(td, "labels/blobs")) plot(x, all = TRUE)

title: Introduction to `rome`
text: remote OME-ZARR files, can use paws.storage::s3 client read data directly S3 bucket without downloading first:
code: library(paws) s3_client <- paws.storage::s3(   config = list(     credentials = list(anonymous = TRUE),     region = "auto",     endpoint = "https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk"   ) ) x <- ome_read(   "https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk/idr/zarr/v0.4/idr0076A/10501752.zarr",   s3_client = s3_client, ) plot(x, all = TRUE)

title: Introduction to `rome`
text: rome also provides utilities writing OME-ZARR images OME-NGFF versions 0.4 0.5.  Users can also define scaling factors image pyramids. scalefactors length three, pyramid four scales. Eac scale factor vector defines scale factor relative previous scale.
code: library(EBImage) img_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_RGB.png", package = "rome") img <- readImage(img_file)  # write image pyramid ome_img <- ome_write(img,                      path = tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr"),                      axes = c("x", "y", "c"),                      version = "0.4",                      storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64, 64, 1))) plot(ome_img, 1) ## Only the first frame of the image stack is displayed. ## To display all frames use 'all = TRUE'. ome_img <- ome_write(img,                      path = tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr"),                      axes = c("x", "y", "c"),                      version = "0.5",                      scalefactors = c(2, 2, 3),                      storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64, 64, 1)))

title: Introduction to `rome`
text: OME-ZARR label pyramids can generated way. first create label data using EBImage first. can now write label pyramid. Arguments similar images written.  Additional metadata information labels can provided using label_metadata argument. path already includes image pyramid, define name (e.g. blobs) label pyramid associated image.
code: library(EBImage)  # read the first frame of image nuc <- readImage(system.file("images", "nuclei.tif", package = "EBImage")) nuc <- getFrames(nuc)[[1]]  # threshold using otsu's method nuc_th <- nuc > otsu(nuc) ome_nuc_th <- ome_write(nuc_th,                         path = tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr"),                         version = "0.4",                         scalefactors = c(2, 2, 3),                         storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64, 64)),                         type = "label") plot(ome_nuc_th, 3) # write label, version 0.4 ome_nuc_th <- ome_write(nuc_th,                         path = tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr"),                         version = "0.4",                         scalefactors = c(2, 2, 3),                         storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64, 64)),                         type = "label",                         label_name = "blobs",                         label_metadata = list(                           colors = list(                             list(`label-value` = 1, rgba = list(255, 255, 255, 255)),                             list(`label-value` = 2, rgba = list(0, 255, 255, 128))                           ),                           properties = list(                             list(`label-value` = 1, class = "A"),                             list(`label-value` = 2, class = "B")                           )                         )) td <- tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr")  # write image pyramid ome_nuc <- ome_write(nuc,                      path = td,                      version = "0.4",                      storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64, 64)))  ome_nuc_th <- ome_write(nuc_th,                         path = td,                         version = "0.4",                         scalefactors = c(2, 2, 3),                         storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64, 64)),                         type = "label",                         label_name = "blobs") ## An image pyramid was found at '/tmp/RtmpfYBXGj/file1ced31925e16.ome.zarr', writing labels to 'labels/blobs' # plot layout(matrix(1:2, nrow = 1)) plot(ome_nuc, 3) plot(ome_nuc_th, 3)

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: rome minimal R package read write multiscale OME-ZARR (OME-NGFF) images. package also provides helper methods manipulate resulting ome_zarr objects way one manipulate traditional arrays R. example, can subset ome_zarr object using [ operator, subsetting applied levels multiscale OME-Zarr object.

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: can install development version rome like :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("Huber-group-EMBL/rome")

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: basic example shows read OME-ZARR image version 0.4. default, read performed lazily using ZarrArray.  remote OME-ZARR files, can use paws.storage::s3 client read data directly S3 bucket without downloading first:
code: library(rome) library(utils) omezarrzip <- system.file("extdata", "test_ngff_image_v04.ome.zarr.zip", package = "rome") dir.create(td <- tempfile()) unzip(omezarrzip, exdir = td) x <- ome_read(td) plot(x, 1) library(paws) s3_client <- paws.storage::s3(   config = list(     credentials = list(anonymous = TRUE),     region = "auto",     endpoint = "https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk"   ) ) x <- ome_read(   "https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk/idr/zarr/v0.4/idr0076A/10501752.zarr",    s3_client = s3_client, )

title: Minimal R OME-Zarr Reader
text: rome also provides utilities writing OME-ZARR images OME-NGFF versions 0.4 0.5. package also supports writing pyramids using scalfactors argument.
code: # read image library(EBImage) img_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_RGB.png", package="rome") img <- readImage(img_file)  # write image pyramid ome_img <- ome_write(img,                      path = tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr"),                      version = "0.4",                      scalefactors = c(2,2,3),                      storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64,64,1))) plot(ome_img) #> Only the first frame of the image stack is displayed. #> To display all frames use 'all = TRUE'.

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
code: ome_read(path, s3_client = NULL, lazy = TRUE, validate = TRUE)

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
text: path Path OME-Zarr file. s3_client Object created paws.storage::s3(). required file S3. Leave NULL file local storage. lazy Logical. TRUE (default), use ZarrArray read data lazily. FALSE, read data memory using Rarr. data can fit memory, setting lazy = FALSE may result better performance. validate Logical.TRUE (default), validate OME-Zarr file.

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
text: object ome_zarr (OME-Zarr) class representing image label pyramid.

title: Read a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_read
code: omezarrzip <- system.file("extdata",                           "test_ngff_image_v04.ome.zarr.zip",                           package = "rome") dir.create(td <- tempfile()) unzip(omezarrzip, exdir = td) x <- ome_read(td)

title: Validate a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_validate
text: function used side-effect return type OME-Zarr schema (image, label), otherwise invoke error passed invalid OME-Zarr file

title: Validate a multiscale OME-Zarr file — ome_validate
code: omezarrzip <- system.file("extdata",                           "test_ngff_image_v04.ome.zarr.zip",                           package = "rome") dir.create(td <- tempfile()) unzip(omezarrzip, exdir = td) ome_validate(td) #> [1] "image"

title: ome_write — ome_write
text: Writes ome image zarr path according ome-zarr specification

title: ome_write — ome_write
code: ome_write(   image,   path = "/",   axes = NULL,   scalefactors = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   version = c("0.4", "0.5"),   storage_options = NULL,   type = c("image", "label"),   label_name = NULL,   label_metadata = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'character' ome_write(   image,   path = "/",   axes = NULL,   scalefactors = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   version = c("0.4", "0.5"),   storage_options = NULL,   type = c("image", "label"),   label_name = NULL,   label_metadata = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'array' ome_write(   image,   path = "/",   axes = NULL,   scalefactors = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   version = c("0.4", "0.5"),   storage_options = NULL,   type = c("image", "label"),   label_name = NULL,   label_metadata = NULL )  # S4 method for class 'Image' ome_write(   image,   path = "/",   axes = NULL,   scalefactors = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   version = c("0.4", "0.5"),   storage_options = NULL,   type = c("image", "label"),   label_name = NULL,   label_metadata = NULL )

title: ome_write — ome_write
text: image n-dimensional (path ) array representing image data (1<n<6) path path writing ome.zarr axes character vector specifying axes image (e.g. c("t", "c", "z", "y", "x")) scalefactors Scale factors apply construct multiscale image. Importantly, scale factor relative previous scale factor. example, scale factors c(2, 2, 2), returned multiscale image 4 scales. version OME-ZARR version (0.4 0.5), lower versions supported writing. storage_options list storage options zarr array (e.g. chunks) type type OME pyramid written: 'image' (default) 'label'. label_name name label data. label_metadata label metadata added attributes.

title: ome_write — ome_write
text: object ome_zarr (OME-Zarr) class representing image label pyramid.

title: ome_write — ome_write
code: library(EBImage) nuc <- readImage(system.file("images", "nuclei.tif", package="EBImage")) nuc <- getFrames(nuc)[[1]] td <- tempfile(fileext = ".ome.zarr")  # write image pyramid ome_nuc <- ome_write(nuc,                      path = td,                      version = "0.4",                      storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64,64)))  # nuclei segmentation using otsu's method nuc_th = nuc > otsu(nuc)  # write label pyramid ome_nuc_th <- ome_write(nuc_th,                         path = td,                         version = "0.4",                         scalefactors = c(2,2,3),                         storage_options = list(chunk_dim = c(64,64)),                         label_name = "nuclei_segmentation",                         type = "label") #> An image pyramid was found at '/tmp/RtmpWKFMiR/file1be4c50f7f.ome.zarr', writing labels to 'labels/nuclei_segmentation'

title: Subset an ome-zarr object — [.ome_zarr
text: subset object ome_zarr (OME-Zarr) class representing image label pyramid.

title: Subset an ome-zarr object — [.ome_zarr
text: first image subsetted using provided indices, resulting dimensions used subset remaining levels, conserving scaling factor across levels

title: Subset an ome-zarr object — [.ome_zarr
code: omezarrzip <- system.file("extdata",                           "test_ngff_image_v04.ome.zarr.zip",                           package = "rome") dir.create(td <- tempfile()) unzip(omezarrzip, exdir = td) x <- ome_read(td) y <- x[1:5,1:5] plot(y, level = 2)
